message:
feat(export): Cx-first export_jsonl (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Add Cx-first sibling to the sole public async export entry
point. Pre-flight checkpoint gates the expensive storage query
(one of: export_segments, export_gaps, get_events,
export_workflows, export_sessions, get_audit_actions, or
export_reservations + per-workflow get_step_logs).

Addition (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `export_jsonl_with_cx(cx, storage, opts, writer)` — Cx-first
    parallel of `export_jsonl`. Pre-flight `cx.checkpoint()?`
    gates the storage query. For uncancelled cx the behavior
    is identical to the legacy function (delegates after the
    checkpoint).

Per-record cancellation is out of scope: once the storage
query returns a `Vec<Record>`, the write loop is CPU-only
serde + I/O. Checkpointing between records would add ceremony
without meaningful cancellation value — storage query cost
dominates export time on large tables.

Regression test (new) `export_jsonl_with_cx_matches_legacy`:

  * Mirrors the `export_segments_to_buffer` fixture (pane + 1
    segment with content "cx-test-content").
  * Calls `export_jsonl_with_cx(&cx, ...)` with
    `ExportKind::Segments`.
  * Asserts:
    - count == 1
    - output line count == 2 (header + 1 record)
    - header record_count == 1
    - record content matches

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * export:: — 174 passed / 0 failed (tick 88: 173 -> tick 89:
    174, +1 new parity test)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (29.09s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Cx-first export.rs trail COMPLETE — sole public async method
has a cx sibling.

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
